title: andropogon nutans var. submuticus
intro: Andropogon nutans var. submuticus, commonly known as the Red Grama or Red Indiangrass, is a type of grass species that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The Red Grama has been reported to have various health benefits, including lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and exhibiting antioxidant properties that can help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Andropogon nutans var. submuticus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
